title: rubus × fraternalis
intro: Rubus × fraternalis, also known as the hybrid raspberry or boysenberry, is a type of bramble that is a cross between the European raspberry (Rubus idaeus) and the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis). The plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its high content of vitamins C and K, as well as its rich supply of phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Rubus × fraternalis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Rubus × fraternalis can be prepared as a tea, juice, or jam, and it is also used as a dietary supplement, offering a range of potential health benefits when consumed in moderation.
